config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atas segalanya, mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai tanpa mengorbankan hak dasar seseorang secara sembarangan. Dari sudut pandang saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru yang bisa menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should usually come first, because once society is allowed to override them too easily, abuse becomes very hard to stop. From an American perspective, I am especially wary of arguments that sound like "the greater good" when they can be used to silence minorities, justify surveillance, or strip away basic freedoms. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case, because public safety, public health, and basic order do require some limits. But the burden should be on society to prove those limits are necessary, narrow, and temporary, not on the individual to constantly defend their freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju dengan pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat masyarakat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, dan itu berbahaya karena bisa menutupi ketidakadilan terhadap minoritas atau orang yang lemah. Memang ada situasi seperti kesehatan publik, keamanan, atau bencana ketika pembatasan hak bisa dibenarkan, tetapi harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Jadi, yang paling benar menurut saya bukan menomorsatukan masyarakat secara mutlak, melainkan menjaga keseimbangan yang tegas dengan hak dasar sebagai batasnya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can become a blank check for abuse. Agent A is right that public health, safety, and emergencies can justify some limits, but those limits have to be narrow and accountable, or they start swallowing the very freedoms they claim to protect. I do not trust any system that tells individuals to give up core rights first and ask questions later, especially when minorities are the ones most likely to pay the price. For me, society is strongest when it serves individual rights, not when it treats them as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu mudah untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang sebenarnya tidak adil, terutama bagi kelompok kecil atau yang suaranya lemah. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti bencana, kesehatan publik, atau keamanan yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan hak, tetapi itu harus benar-benar terbatas, transparan, dan bisa diuji. Jadi, bagi saya, masyarakat yang baik bukan yang menekan hak individu, melainkan yang menjaga kepentingan bersama tanpa mengorbankan hak dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, the phrase "society first" often sounds like a convenient excuse for government overreach, and history shows that the people who get hurt first are usually minorities, dissenters, and anyone without much power. I do think there are real exceptions in emergencies like public health or immediate danger, but those limits should be tightly defined, temporary, and open to challenge. If we make individual rights easy to override, we are not strengthening society; we are making it easier for abuse to hide behind a noble slogan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
